description:
Offered: Spring
The course explores the ways gender plays a role in economic life in expanding or constraining opportunities available to individuals in the U.S. and in the developing world. Topics will include: gender division of labor in paid and unpaid spheres, gendered patterns of employment and unemployment, and discrimination in labor markets.
Prerequisite(s): ECON 1100 or ECON 1200
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, DP
